label: IAM Engineer
description: Designs, implements, and operates identity and access management solutions covering identity governance, privileged access management, authentication, federation, SSO, and identity platform administration.

paths.name: Microsoft Identity Track
paths.rationale: For IAM engineers in Microsoft-first organizations. Entra ID is the dominant enterprise identity platform — the Microsoft certification stack is increasingly required for identity engineering roles at Microsoft shops.
pros: SC-300 is listed as required or preferred in most Microsoft identity engineering job postings
Low cost compared to vendor platform certifications
Directly applicable to the dominant enterprise identity platform
AZ-500 provides the security engineering context around identity

prerequisites.experience: Prior Active Directory or Entra ID administration experience is strongly recommended. AZ-900 before SC-300 is advisable.

cert_sequence.notes: Microsoft Identity and Access Administrator — the primary Entra ID engineering credential. Covers Conditional Access, identity governance, privileged identity management, external identities, and hybrid identity. Study with John Savill's Microsoft Azure Master Class and Microsoft Learn. Hands-on labs in a real Entra ID tenant are essential — use a free Microsoft 365 developer tenant.

Azure Security Engineer — adds the security engineering context around identity. Covers Defender for Identity, Key Vault, and the broader Azure security posture that identity controls sit within. SC-300 + AZ-500 is the most recognized Microsoft identity security combination.

Microsoft Cybersecurity Architect — the strategic capstone covering Zero Trust architecture, identity-centric security design, and security operations architecture. Opens senior architect and lead engineer roles. With SC-300 and AZ-500 completed, the architectural perspective feels grounded in operational reality.

paths.name: Okta Identity Track
paths.rationale: For IAM engineers on the Okta platform — the leading independent identity provider used across cloud-first organizations. Okta certifications are increasingly required for identity engineering roles at Okta shops.
pros: Okta Professional and Consultant are widely required at Okta customer organizations
CIDPRO adds vendor-neutral identity governance foundation
Okta market share in cloud-first organizations is substantial

prerequisites.experience: Prior Okta administration or similar identity platform experience is strongly recommended. Okta's free developer tenant (developer.okta.com) is essential for hands-on practice.

cert_sequence.notes: IDPro's vendor-neutral identity professional certification — covers identity fundamentals, digital identity concepts, IAM program structure, and identity protocols independent of any platform. Good foundational credential before platform-specific Okta certifications. IDPro is building recognition as the professional body for identity practitioners.

Okta Certified Administrator — the Okta entry-level credential covering platform administration, user management, application integration, and policy configuration. Take Okta's official training and use the developer tenant extensively for hands-on practice.

Okta Certified Technical Architect — Okta's advanced technical credential covering complex integration design, advanced federation scenarios, and multi-tenant architecture. A significant step up from OCA in both depth and difficulty. At this level you're designing Okta implementations for complex enterprise requirements, not just administering a configured tenant.

paths.name: Privileged Access Management Track
paths.rationale: For IAM engineers specializing in PAM — the highest-risk segment of identity management. CyberArk dominates the PAM market; CyberArk certifications are near-mandatory for PAM engineering roles.
pros: CyberArk Sentry and Guardian are required by name at most CyberArk customer organizations
PAM specialization commands premium compensation — high demand, low supply of skilled PAM engineers
HashiCorp Vault adds secrets management for DevOps and cloud PAM scope
CIDPRO adds vendor-neutral identity governance foundation
cons: Platform-specific — limited transferability outside CyberArk environments
CyberArk Guardian is advanced — requires substantial platform experience

knowledge: Privileged access concepts and PAM methodology
Active Directory and Windows server administration
prerequisites.experience: Windows server and Active Directory administration experience is strongly recommended before CyberArk certification.

cert_sequence.notes: Vendor-neutral identity professional foundation. Establishes the IAM framework context that PAM sits within.

CyberArk Defender PAM — the CyberArk entry-level PAM credential covering Vault administration, account management, and core PAM platform operation. CyberArk's official training is the primary preparation resource. Access to a CyberArk lab environment (either employer-provided or CyberArk's training environment) is essential.

CyberArk Sentry PAM — the intermediate credential covering advanced platform configuration, PSM, CPM, and complex privileged session management scenarios. The credential most commonly listed in CyberArk engineer job postings. Requires hands-on CyberArk experience — cannot be passed through study alone.

HashiCorp Vault Professional — adds secrets management and cloud-native PAM coverage that extends your PAM skills into DevOps and cloud environments where CyberArk's traditional privileged account vault model needs supplementing. Vault is the dominant secrets management platform in cloud-native and DevOps environments. The practical exam requires real Vault configuration and policy writing skill.
